response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
I think it's really important for people like us teenagers to learn how to handle fake news online. When I was younger, my parents always told me about critical thinking, but now that everything is digitalized, we need new tools too.

Social media has become part of everyday life even though sometimes I feel overwhelmed by all the misinformation out there. Being depressed myself makes this harder because negative stuff can really affect you if you're not careful. But my dad got a PhD and he always talks about how education helps in filtering lies from truth, so maybe that's something to focus on.

I also like football (as my hobby) but it doesn't help much against fake content online.
response_parsed.tone: Concerned yet optimistic, with references to personal experiences and positive coping mechanisms.
citations: Personal experience as a depressed teenager
